title: Paying for education
description: Practice understanding of ways to pay for education, differentiate among different types of aid, describe scholarships and scholarship scams.

assessment_items.question_content:
Many people use student loans to pay for their education, but there are other ways to cover the costs.

**Which of these sources can help pay for education?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Many people use scholarships to pay for their education, but there are other ways to cover the costs.

**Which of these sources can help pay for education?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Going to school can be expensive, but there are many ways to fund higher education. 

**Which of these can be used to pay for education?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The best type of financial aid you can get is the one you do not have to pay back. 

**Which of these types of financial aid do not require repayment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The best type of financial aid you can get is the one you do not have to pay back. 

**Which of these types of financial aid do not require repayment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Some types of financial aid require you to pay back the money you used to fund your education. 

**Which of these types of financial aid require repayment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Unlike grants, scholarships are typically merit-based.

**What does the *merit* in a merit-based scholarship typically refer to?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Unlike grants, scholarships are typically merit-based.

**Which of these would be considered a merit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Athletic scholarships are one of many ways to earn money for school, but are not the only merit-based scholarship.

**Which of the following could be considered for a merit-based scholarship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: competing in regional finals in orchestra
volunteering in a local food bank

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently got an email from Scholarships for America, informing you that your good grades qualify you for a $\$5{,}000$ scholarship. In order to receive the scholarship funds, you have to provide your bank account for deposit.

**What is described in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

As a member of the varsity team, you receive an email from the Scholarship for Young Athletes Foundation. They offer to send your details to universities with athletic scholarships for $\$19.99$.

**What is described in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A college recruiter who watched you play volleyball calls you and asks for your email to send a scholarship offer. They require a credit or debit card for registration but assure you there's no fee.

**What is described in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The financial aid office at your prospective school informs you that you qualify for direct subsidized student loans.

**Who acts as the lender for direct subsidized student loans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your financial aid letter states that you qualify for subsidized and unsubsidized student loans.

**Who is the lender for direct unsubsidized student loans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The financial aid office at your prospective school informs you that you qualify for private student loans.

**Who is the lender for private student loans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Interest, as a cost of borrowing, also applies to student loans.

**Arrange the students loans below from *lowest* to *highest* interest.**



[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Subsidized and unsubsidized loans are both types of federal student loans.
Federal student loans have *lower* interest rates than private loans.
The term *subsidized* indicates that the federal government is covering the interest while the student is in school. This makes them the lowest-interest loan overall.

assessment_items.question_content:

**Arrange the students loans below from *highest* to *lowest* interest.**



[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Subsidized and unsubsidized loans are both types of federal student loans.

assessment_items.question_content:
Interest, as a cost of borrowing, also applies to student loans.

**Which student loans have the highest costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Some people who take out student loans struggle to repay them.

**Which of these factors make repayment more difficult?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Some people who take out student loans struggle to repay them.

**Which of these factors make repayment more difficult?**
